global_rules: PRE-FLIGHT CHECKLIST: 1. Modal Check (body.modal-active). 2. Use State Proxy. 3. Check Recipes. 4. WAIT before ACT.
Modal Check: FORBIDDEN to interact if `document.body.classList.contains('modal-active')` or `.modal-overlay` is visible.
Strict Flags First: On Linux, use `agent-browser <cmd> --session-name <name> --args '--no-sandbox'`.
Wait then Act: ALWAYS use `wait <selector>` or `sleep 1` before `click`, `type`, or `scrollintoview`.
State-Proxy Verification: Call `node scripts/lib/state-proxy.mjs get` after EVERY action to verify mutation.

BATCHED PROBES: Mandate that a single `agent-browser eval` call captures all 4 core metrics (Credits, Hunger, Sanity, Location) and any specific local selectors (e.g., job availability) in one pass to minimize turns.
Prefer targeted eval probes that return compact JSON over document-wide text dumps.
Prefer stable data-testid selectors first; verify the resulting game state after any job application or purchase.
Do not trust a successful click response by itself; confirm the intended before/after gameplay mutation for travel, apply, shopping, and work-shift actions.
If continuity is missing, capture one screenshot, confirm the reset state, and restore the latest checkpoint before replaying from onboarding.
Treat broad DOM inventories and full-page text dumps as debug-only fallbacks.

session_recovery.goal: Recover authoritative play when the named browser session opens on onboarding or has no save state.
steps: Run one compact eval probe to confirm the current URL, whether onboarding is visible, and whether localStorage contains jones_fastlane_save.
If no save is present and a checkpoint path is known, run workflow:phase11:checkpoint:import before taking gameplay actions.
Re-check the same compact state fields after import.
success_signals: the UI is no longer on onboarding
turn and player stats match the expected checkpoint

preferred_actions: Click the named city button by its `data-testid` first; city travel cards are now semantic `<button>` controls rather than custom clickable divs.
Use the location-specific `city-travel-card-*` selector instead of broad text scans or full DOM dumps whenever the destination is known.
Only fall back to targeted DOM evaluation if the expected `city-travel-card-*` control is absent or the post-click state verification fails.
verification: Check the active location label or dashboard title.
Check player time after travel when the destination should cost time.
Treat a successful click response without location or time change as a failed travel attempt.

apply_job.goal: Secure a labor shift job such as Sanitation-T3.
preferred_actions: Call `agent-browser scrollintoview` on the targeted job button before any direct click.
Click the button selector first once it is in view.
Immediately verify CURRENT SHIFT or persisted `careerLevel` before continuing the slice.
verification: Check CURRENT SHIFT or the persisted game state for the selected job id.
Treat a successful click response without state mutation as a failed apply attempt.
fallbacks: Re-query the specific job card and button only, not the whole DOM.
If the first direct click reports success without state change, re-run `scrollintoview`, click once more, and verify `careerLevel` or CURRENT SHIFT again.

verification: Confirm credits changed by the expected amount.
Confirm hunger or sanity moved in the expected direction.
Treat a successful click response without the expected stat deltas as a failed purchase attempt.

work_shift.goal: Execute a labor shift once the current job is active.
preferred_actions: Find the visible WORK SHIFT control.
After each shift, re-check credits, time, hunger, sanity, and whether a turn summary or event modal interrupted the route.
verification: Credits increase by the shift yield.
Time drops by the expected shift cost.
Any forced event or summary modal is detected immediately.
Treat a successful click response without the expected credits, time, or modal change as a failed shift attempt.

turn_summary_probe.goal: Capture the authoritative end-of-week state with minimal context cost.
turn_summary_probe.mandate: A single `agent-browser eval` call SHOULD capture all 4 core metrics (Credits, Hunger, Sanity, Location) and any specific local selectors (e.g., job availability) in one pass.
